skill.name: inngest-handler
skill.description: Create and manage Inngest functions for reliable background jobs, workflows, and scheduled tasks.
skill.summary: Create and manage Inngest functions for reliable background jobs, workflows, and scheduled tasks.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks, TypeScript examples, and descriptive text as command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance. Review found documentation-only Inngest guidance with no runnable scripts, shell execution, credential access, network exfiltration, or prompt-injection attempts. Risk is low because the flagged patterns are false positives, and publication is acceptable.

low_findings.title: Static command-execution detections are Markdown examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code, fenced TypeScript examples, and a TypeScript template literal. The files contain guidance for Inngest functions, not executable shell commands or Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside Markdown documentation and TypeScript snippets. No shell command execution primitive or runnable skill script is present.

low_findings.title: Weak-cryptography detection is a text false positive
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding points to the skill description line. That line describes background jobs and workflows, and it does not mention or implement cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line contains only metadata text for the skill description. There is no cryptographic API, hash function, or encryption code at that location.

low_findings.title: System-reconnaissance detections are workflow examples
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings point to an Inngest priority example and a validation branch. These lines do not inspect the host system, enumerate files, read environment data, or collect machine information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is documentation for Inngest flow control and error handling. No system information commands or APIs are referenced.

content.value_statement: Background jobs often fail when retries, delays, and side effects are handled inconsistently. This skill guides Inngest function design with durable steps, sleeps, event waits, and registration rules.
actual_capabilities: Define Inngest functions with stable event triggers and step IDs.
Structure database writes, API calls, and non-deterministic work inside durable steps.
Use Inngest sleeps, event waits, retries, rate limits, throttling, debounce, and priority settings.
Design failure handlers that run cleanup and notification steps after retries fail.
Register new functions in the expected Inngest functions index file.
limitations: Provides guidance and examples, not a runnable code generator.
Assumes a TypeScript project that already uses Inngest.
Does not configure Inngest accounts, secrets, deployments, or webhooks.
Does not validate business-specific retry, timeout, or rate-limit values.

use_cases.title: Create order workflows
use_cases.description: Build multi-step order processing that validates data, waits for payment, and cancels stale orders.

use_cases.title: Stabilize serverless jobs
use_cases.description: Replace fragile timers and ad hoc retry logic with durable Inngest sleeps, retries, and step boundaries.

use_cases.title: Review workflow patterns
use_cases.description: Check existing Inngest functions for side effects outside steps, unstable IDs, and missing failure handling.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic function
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Inngest function for [event name] that performs [main task]. Use durable steps and explain where it should be registered.

prompt_templates.title: Add delays and waits
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Inngest workflow that waits [duration] after [event], then waits for [follow-up event] before continuing.

prompt_templates.title: Improve reliability
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Inngest function for side effects outside steps, unstable step IDs, unsafe timers, and missing returned step data.

prompt_templates.title: Design advanced flow control
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an Inngest workflow for [business process] using concurrency, rate limits, debounce, priority, retries, and failure cleanup.

output_examples.input: Create a payment follow-up workflow for a new order event.
output_examples.output: A recommended workflow with validation, a durable payment wait, timeout handling, cancellation logic, and registration guidance.

output_examples.input: Review a function that uses timers and writes to the database before a step.
output_examples.output: A review that flags the timer and external side effect, then recommends Inngest sleep and step-wrapped database operations.

output_examples.input: Add rate limiting and failure cleanup to a customer sync workflow.
output_examples.output: A production plan that sets rate limits, retry behavior, cleanup steps, and an administrator notification path.

best_practices: Put database writes, API calls, randomness, and date logic inside named Inngest steps.
Use stable step IDs and return values from steps when later workflow stages need them.
Register every function through the project functions index so Inngest can discover it.
anti_patterns: Using setTimeout or setInterval for workflow delays in serverless functions.
Performing side effects before the first step or between steps without step.run.
Generating step IDs unpredictably, which can break memoization and retries.

faq.question: Does this skill create runnable Inngest code?
faq.answer: It provides patterns and examples that an assistant can apply when writing or reviewing Inngest functions.

faq.question: Which projects does it fit best?
faq.answer: It fits TypeScript applications that already use Inngest for background jobs, workflows, or scheduled tasks.

faq.question: Can it replace queue infrastructure design?
faq.answer: No. It guides Inngest function structure, but architecture decisions still need project-specific review.

faq.question: Does it cover retries?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains automatic retries, custom retry counts, non-retriable errors, and failure handlers.

faq.question: Does it cover rate limits and throttling?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for rate limits, throttling, debounce, concurrency, and priority settings.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation-only examples and no confirmed malicious behavior.
